title: pogogyne douglasii var. typica
intro: Pogogyne douglasii var. typica, a subspecies of the Douglas' fog-giant plant, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of wounds. The herbal preparation of Pogogyne douglasii var. typica typically involves the leaves and stems being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
